description: Derived from a full year (2021) of raw + technician-corrected data — 32,613 sonde records (93% completeness). Urban stream with strong diurnal cycles, occasional storm-driven turbidity spikes (>500 NTU), and one major sensor failure period in July-August 2021. pH and DO sensors are well-behaved; SpC and turbidity show periodic fouling. Note: this station's logger uses NaN (not -99999) for missing data.

temperature.notes: Observed 42-86°F over the year. Range_min=32 (freezing) is a safety floor.

specific_conductivity.notes: Stable urban baseline ~0.1 mS/cm. Persistence 48 (12 hr) is fine here — flatlines are much shorter than KINA's. Range_max 0.5 will catch the major fault events where sensor jumps to 100+ mS/cm.

pH.notes: Observed range 5.94-8.63 in corrected data. Range_min 5.5 catches out-of-range raw values like 4.09. The technician rejected ~3,400 raw outliers.

dissolved_oxygen.notes: DO well-behaved here (only 0.3% corrected). Range_max 16 leaves room for supersaturation; the lone 18.58 spike was likely a glitch and would be flagged.

turbidity.notes: Allow negative values down to -15 (sonde fouling artifact technician left in). High max_rate (500) because real storm pulses hit 230 NTU per 15-min step. Range_max 1500 catches the worst fault spikes.

stage.notes: Stage column may be entirely null in some exports of this station — verify before relying on it as a covariate.

data_quality_notes.logger_sentinel: NaN (not -99999). No numeric sentinel filtering needed at this station.

source: Derived from COLA_SMIB_2021_CORRECTED.xlsx + Campbell PakBus Logger export for calendar year 2021
